title: unable to start a container
body:
### Issue Description

when staring a podman container using podman-compose up -d, i get the following error:

Error: unable to start container "1658c6e0bc891751926b1f968abb67ff5963c37dc4454e361de1409dadc06aa6": rootless netns: mount "/tmp/containers/networks/rootless-netns/run" to "/run": no such file or directory

i remove the container+pod, make the directory /tmp/containers/networks/rootless-netns/run, and then run it again. it fails again with the same error message, and the directory that i just created is gone!

here is my podman-compose file:
```yml
version: "3"
services:
  garage:
    image: docker.io/dxflrs/garage:v1.0.0
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - /etc/garage.toml:/etc/garage.toml
      - /var/lib/garage/meta:/var/lib/garage/meta
      - /var/lib/garage/data:/var/lib/garage/data
    ports:
      - 3901:3901
```

here is the output of `podmancompose up -d`:
```
WARN[0000] Using cgroups-v1 which is deprecated in favor of cgroups-v2 with Podman v5 and will be removed in a future version. Set environment vari
able `PODMAN_IGNORE_CGROUPSV1_WARNING` to hide this warning.
051312a300621494cd49d2c617c3f0a7e9adb955678c01d91f3031379ef9912c
WARN[0000] Using cgroups-v1 which is deprecated in favor of cgroups-v2 with Podman v5 and will be removed in a future version. Set environment vari
able `PODMAN_IGNORE_CGROUPSV1_WARNING` to hide this warning.
WARN[0000] aardvark-dns binary not found, container dns will not be enabled
c79d146680fe19b950fa461e312b2d0c0eac5a3b8c235d19bb42450dad84b32b
WARN[0000] Using cgroups-v1 which is deprecated in favor of cgroups-v2 with Podman v5 and will be removed in a future version. Set environment vari
able `PODMAN_IGNORE_CGROUPSV1_WARNING` to hide this warning.
WARN[0001] aardvark-dns binary not found, container dns will not be enabled
Error: unable to start container "c79d146680fe19b950fa461e312b2d0c0eac5a3b8c235d19bb42450dad84b32b": rootless netns: mount "/tmp/containers/network
s/rootless-netns/run" to "/run": no such file or directory
```

i do not know much about network namespaces.

i did not invoke podman/podman-compose with sudo.

i am using void linux glibc

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. copy this podman compose file
2. run podman-compose -d up
3. it does create a container, but fails to run giving the aforementioned output


### Describe the results you received

container fails to run giving the aforementioned output

### Describe the results you expected

container should be created and process should be running

### Podman in a container

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details: None

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

ocassionally panics on null dereference, forgot to get the log output, but it was some function with a name similar to `getHost`
